summary: Partially decompiled the early/non-RNG portion of the Figure Get stage file by moving several grFigureGet functions and stage data out of asm into src/melee/gr/grfigureget.c, adding a matching header, and building the new C object before the remaining asm object. The author explicitly noted this was equivalent to early work done for other Figure files and that the remaining work was RNG-related functions not attempted in this PR.
what_changed: Removed the matched early grFigureGet functions and related data from asm/melee/gr/grfigureget.s while leaving later functions, starting at grFigureGet_802196F0 and beyond, in assembly.
Added src/melee/gr/grfigureget.c with C implementations for no-op stage callbacks, stage initialization, cleanup/boolean callback stubs, gobj creation/setup, and animation setup.
Recreated grFigureGet_803E7D00 as a two-entry static StageCallbacks array and grFigureGet_803E7D34 as StageData for stage id 0x26 using the file path "/GrNFg.dat".
Added src/melee/gr/grfigureget.h with static prototypes for the newly C-matched local functions and includes for gr/stage.h and baselib/gobj.h.
Updated obj_files.mk to add $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grfigureget.c.o before the remaining asm/melee/gr/grfigureget.s.o, enabling a split C/asm object arrangement for partial matching.

Ported grFigureGet_80219534 to initialize grFigureGet_804D6AC0 from Ground_801C49F8, set stage_info.unk8C.b4 to 0 and b5 to 1, spawn/setup gobj ids 0 and 1, then call Ground_801C39C0 and Ground_801C3BB4.

Ported grFigureGet_802195CC to call Ground_801C14D0, configure Ground callbacks, set up the GX link with grDisplay_801C5DB0, optionally invoke callback0, optionally install callback2 via HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and report failure through OSReport.
Ported grFigureGet_802196B4 to call grAnime_801C8138 using the Ground user_data map_id.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scoped to clearly matchable early stage boilerplate and explicitly deferred the RNG-related functions, reducing risk while still shrinking the asm file.
Used the common stage-file pattern: StageCallbacks table plus StageData table, then generic Ground_801C14D0/GObj_SetupGXLink/HSD_GObjProc setup logic.
Preserved partial decompilation by placing the new C object before the remaining asm object in obj_files.mk instead of trying to convert the full file at once.
Represented the asm value 0xC0000000 in the callbacks table as (1 << 30) | (1 << 31), making the flag intent/bit layout more apparent while preserving the value.
Matched bitfield writes from rlwimi/lbz/stb assembly as stage_info.unk8C.b4 and stage_info.unk8C.b5 assignments, reusing the existing StageInfo layout rather than raw byte manipulation.
Reconstructed embedded assembly data strings as normal C string literals, including "/GrNFg.dat", "grfigureget.c", and the OSReport format string.
decomp_lessons: Figure/stage files can often be partially matched by first converting the boilerplate StageData, StageCallbacks, no-op callbacks, setup callbacks, and animation setup while leaving specialized gameplay or RNG routines in asm.
When a stage file has a large callbacks table in .data, matching the table in C can also require forward externs for still-asm functions referenced by the table.
Partial source/asm splitting depends on object order in obj_files.mk; the C object must be added while the remaining asm object stays in the build for unmatched symbols.
Ground user_data fields x8_callback, xC_callback, x1C_callback and map_id can map directly to common stage setup patterns seen in other gr* files.
Assembly failure paths using OSReport can often reveal source filename and line number constants; preserving them as literals can be necessary for matching.
Void no-op functions and false-returning callbacks are valid low-risk starting points for stage decompilation because they often match simple blr or li r3,0; blr assembly exactly.
naming_conventions: Retained address-based symbol names such as grFigureGet_80219534, grFigureGet_802195CC, and grFigureGet_803E7D34 because no semantic names were established in the PR.
Used grFigureGet_803E7D00 for the StageCallbacks array and grFigureGet_803E7D34 for the exported StageData, matching the existing assembly labels.
Used local variable names gobj, gp, callbacks, and gobj_id for common HSD_GObj/Ground setup code.
Header guard used the project-style lowercase underscore form _grfigureget_h_.
Stage asset path was represented as "/GrNFg.dat", corresponding to the original data bytes for the Figure Get stage file.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted simple blr functions into empty static void functions.
Converted li r3, 0; blr functions into static bool functions returning false.
Converted the assembly table value 0xC0000000 into the equivalent bit expression (1 << 30) | (1 << 31).
Matched stage_info byte bit manipulation by assigning named bitfields stage_info.unk8C.b4 and stage_info.unk8C.b5.

Matched gobj setup flow by preserving call order: Ground_801C14D0, clear Ground callbacks, GObj_SetupGXLink, install callback3 if present, invoke callback0 if present, register callback2 process if present.
Kept unmatched later functions in asm and referenced them from C with extern declarations so the callbacks table and StageData could be compiled in C.
Recreated assembly .data records as typed C StageCallbacks and StageData structures instead of raw .4byte data.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish the RNG-related grFigureGet functions left in asm after grFigureGet_802196F0.
Compare this Figure Get stage setup against other Figure files mentioned by the author for reusable StageData and StageCallbacks matching patterns.
Search for grFigureGet_80219890, grFigureGet_80219898, grFigureGet_80219B0C, grFigureGet_80219C88, and grFigureGet_80219C90 to find the remaining decompilation work.
Check later header cleanups for whether static prototypes in grfigureget.h were retained, moved, or made non-static.
